doom.driver: IAEA issues Ukraine nuclear plant warning after Russia alleges Zaporizhzhia attack.
doom.blurb: EUROPE'S BIGGEST REACTOR IN THE CROSSHAIRS AGAIN
doom.summary: Russia is accusing Ukraine of drone-striking Zaporizhzhia Nuclear Power Plant, prompting the IAEA to issue a 'serious concern' that joins a long and terrifying list of serious IAEA concerns. Meanwhile Israel has seized a 12th-century castle in Lebanon, Trump is 'slowly but surely' tightening Iran deal terms, and Ebola is spreading 'deeply alarmingly' in the Congo — suggesting the universe has decided one active crisis domain is for quitters.

factors.name: Nuclear
factors.detail: IAEA warning over alleged drone attack on Zaporizhzhia keeps Europe's largest nuclear plant in the danger zone, a recurring nightmare that hasn't resolved.

factors.name: Geopolitics
factors.detail: Israel crosses Lebanon's Litani River and seizes Beaufort Castle while Hezbollah fires rockets at Israeli beaches — ceasefire is a word that has lost all meaning.

factors.name: Governance
factors.detail: Trump tightening Iran deal terms with Tehran needing 'days to respond' keeps the ceasefire clock ticking without any structural resolution in sight.

factors.name: Wild Card
factors.detail: MSF calls Ebola spread in DR Congo 'deeply alarming' as the WHO director visits — a biocontainment situation that rarely gets the geopolitical attention it deserves.

factors.name: AI Capability
factors.detail: Anthropic surpassing OpenAI in valuation signals continued AI arms-race momentum, though today's headlines reflect money moving rather than capability leaping.

doom.dalle_prompt: Pixel art, 16-bit SNES style, no text, no antialiasing — weather-dominated scene, dense radioactive-green-tinted fog and industrial haze rolling across the flat Ukrainian steppe, camera positioned at mid-distance ground level looking across the dramatically lowered Kakhovka Reservoir toward the Zaporizhzhia Nuclear Power Plant, the six enormous grey-white VVER-1000 containment domes looming in a single massive linear row through the murk, their rounded crowns dissolving into smoldering orange atmospheric haze above, the long corrugated turbine building stretching beside them with a ragged dark puncture hole visible in its exterior wall — twisted metal framing and exposed insulation catching the last bruised light around the breach — the post-dam-collapse reservoir foreground showing grey-brown mudflats and skeletal dead trees emerging from receding silt-heavy water, military antenna masts and electronic warfare dish arrays barely visible as silhouettes on the plant perimeter through the fog, sandbag fortifications at the fence line half-swallowed by haze, the entire scene filtered through layers of flat oppressive atmosphere that makes the enormous industrial complex feel both deceptively quiet and catastrophically fragile, deep navy shadows dominating the foreground mudflats while smoldering orange bleeds through the fog where the domes meet the sky, heat and threat radiating from the haze itself.

dawn.driver: Intermittent fasting triggers surprising changes in the brain. Losing weight may involve rewiring the gut and the brain at the same time. In a study of obese adults, an intermittent fasting-style diet led to significant weight loss, healthier metabolic markers, a
dawn.blurb: YOUR BRAIN CAN REWIRE ITSELF THROUGH DIET ALONE
dawn.summary: While war dominates the headlines and global tensions simmer, a quieter revolution is unfolding in metabolic medicine. New research shows that intermittent fasting doesn't just change your waistline — it actively rewires gut-brain communication, improving metabolic health at a biological level that scientists are only beginning to map. On a day when so much feels fixed and intractable, this is a reminder that the human body retains a remarkable capacity for transformation.

factors.name: Science
factors.detail: Gut-brain rewiring through diet represents a genuinely new frontier in understanding how lifestyle interventions produce neurological change.

factors.name: Medicine
factors.detail: Omega-3 fish oil research published alongside the fasting study suggests a broader wave of accessible, low-cost metabolic interventions gaining scientific backing.

factors.name: Cooperation
factors.detail: The Shangri-La Dialogue in Singapore brought together defense ministers from dozens of nations, keeping diplomatic channels open despite escalating regional tensions.

factors.name: Human Spirit
factors.detail: A terminal cancer patient is recording farewell lessons for his children on a public podcast, modeling grace and purpose in the face of loss.

factors.name: Wild Card
factors.detail: Victor Wembanyama's Spurs reaching the NBA Finals — a 22-year-old generational talent lifting a historic franchise — is a small burst of pure sporting joy in a heavy news cycle.

dawn.dalle_prompt: Pixel art, 16-bit SNES style, no text, no antialiasing. Extreme close-up filling the entire frame, a single human hand pressed open-palmed against a softly glowing surface, the skin itself rendered in warm golden amber tones as if lit from within, long side-cast shadows stretching across the palm's creases and lifelines. The metaphor: a dense tangle of thin luminous threads — some deep teal, some warm orange — runs visibly beneath the translucent skin of the palm, tracing the branching pathways of veins and nerves, glowing faintly like rewired circuitry awakening. The threads pulse outward from the center of the palm toward the fingertips as if something internal is being rerouted, reconnected, transformed from the inside. The background implied by the edges of the frame is a dim clinical blue-black, the cool darkness of a research environment pressing in from beyond the glow. A faint reflection of amber monitor light catches the surface of the wrist at the frame's lower corner. The dominant palette is deep golden hour amber and warm teal, radiant internal light against surrounding shadow, a body quietly becoming something new.
